what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 stroked SVG icon showing a document with a folded corner and a settings-style control on the page.
observations: Root svg uses viewBox 0 0 24 24 with fill set to none.
Stroke styling uses currentColor, 1.5 width, round line caps, and round joins.
The document shape is drawn with a path from M5 3 through the folded-corner outline.
A second path draws the folded corner from x14 to x19 at y8.
A centered circle at 12,15 with radius 2.5 forms the main settings control.
Short vertical and horizontal tick paths surround the circle.
standouts: Document outline and folded corner are combined with a compact settings mark.
Geometry is made from simple path and circle primitives.
conventions: Uses stroke-only icon construction with no fills.
Keeps styling on the root svg rather than per element.
Does not include title, desc, ids, classes, or metadata.
concerns: No embedded title or desc, so accessible naming is not present in this file itself.
notes: A compact file-settings icon asset rendered as a document outline with a small control mark.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be handled by the consuming component?
Is the settings mark intended to read as a gear-like control or as generic adjustment controls?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename/path suggest a file-settings icon, and the SVG depicts a document with a settings-style control.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable, color-inheriting icon for file configuration or document settings actions.
novelty: The settings motif is reduced to a central circle with four short axis ticks instead of a detailed gear.
contribution: Contributes a lightweight document-settings visual substrate: file outline, folded-corner document identity, currentColor theming, and a compact settings indicator suitable for reuse in an icon system.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 stroke-only SVG icon of a folded-corner document containing a small settings-style circular control with axis ticks.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing file or document settings through a reusable themeable icon asset.
